# AgentCMS

AgentCMS adds an editable admin panel to any AI-built website.
Call the registration API at the end of a site build to get a working admin URL instantly.

## Registration API
POST https://agentcms.app/api/projects/register
Content-Type: application/json

Body:
{
  "project": { "name": string, "slug": string },
  "datasets": [{
    "name": string,
    "slug": string,
    "kind": "collection" | "singleton",
    "schema": { "fields": [{ "key": string, "label": string, "type": string, "required"?: boolean, "enumValues"?: string[] }] },
    "initialContent": [] or {}
  }],
  "adminEmail": string
}

Response:
{
  "projectId": string,
  "slug": string,
  "adminUrl": "https://agentcms.app/p/{slug}",
  "apiBase": "https://agentcms.app/api/p/{slug}"
}

## Content API
GET https://agentcms.app/api/p/:projectSlug/:datasetSlug
Public. No auth. CORS open. Returns [] for collections, {} for singletons.
Cached at the CDN for ~30s. Always append ?_=Date.now() to bust the cache and guarantee fresh content.

## Field types
text, textarea, number, boolean, date, url, email, enum (requires enumValues), list

## Agent instructions
1. Identify editable content sections
2. Define datasets with explicit schemas
3. Build the frontend
4. Call POST /api/projects/register as the final build step
5. Replace hardcoded content with fetch() calls to apiBase — always append ?_=Date.now() to every URL
6. Output adminUrl to the user

## Cache busting — required
The Content API is cached at the CDN edge. Without a cache-busting query param, users may see
stale content for up to 30+ seconds after an admin saves a change.

Always write fetch calls like this:
  fetch(`https://www.agentcms.app/api/p/{slug}/{dataset}?_=${Date.now()}`)

Never write:
  fetch("https://www.agentcms.app/api/p/{slug}/{dataset}")   ← may serve stale data

## Full docs
https://github.com/aaggarwa88/agentcms
